color.$description: Primitive color values for the River Romney design system. These are raw values only — no semantic meaning is attached here. Do not reference these tokens directly in UI code; use semantic tokens (color.background.*, color.foreground.*, color.border.*) instead. Primitives exist so semantic tokens have a single authoritative source for each hex value.

green.$description: A single-hue green ramp from near-black to vivid accent. The palette is intentionally narrow — all UI color comes from this one family plus two neutrals. The warm green undertone is what distinguishes this system from a generic dark theme.

950.$description: Near-black with a warm green undertone. The darkest point in the palette and the foundation of the dark canvas aesthetic. Warmer than pure black (#000000), which reads as sterile. Referenced by: color.background.default, color.foreground.on-action, color.foreground.on-accent.

900.$description: Deep forest green. Visibly distinct from green.950 only on close inspection — creates subtle surface depth without breaking the dark canvas feel. Referenced by: color.background.alt, color.border.default.

600.$description: Muted sage. Mid-range tone that reads clearly above the dark background without demanding attention. The lowest-contrast text color in the system that still meets WCAG AA. Contrast vs green.950: 6.32:1 (AA). Referenced by: color.foreground.muted.

500.$description: Sage green. More saturated than green.600 — a bridge between muted and vivid tones. Not currently mapped to a semantic token; reserved for future component-level tokens or an expanded theme palette.

400.$description: Soft desaturated green. Readable and unobtrusive — neither commanding attention nor fading into the background. Contrast vs green.950: 7.97:1 (AA). Referenced by: color.foreground.alt.

200.$description: Light sage-white. The brightest text tone in the palette. High contrast without the harshness of pure white — the subtle green tint keeps it visually cohesive with the rest of the palette. Contrast vs green.950: 12.26:1 (AAA). Referenced by: color.foreground.default.

accent.$description: Terminal green. The only high-chroma value in the palette — vivid and saturated against the muted ramp. Evokes phosphor displays and CRT terminals, which is intentional. High visual weight; use through semantic tokens that enforce appropriate restraint. Contrast vs green.950: 11.13:1 (AAA). Referenced by: color.background.action, color.background.accent, color.foreground.action, color.foreground.accent.

neutral.$description: Absolute neutrals with no green undertone. Used for edge cases and maximum-contrast needs only. The green palette handles all standard UI color.

white.$description: Pure white. Reserved for maximum-contrast situations such as text overlaid on photography or outside-system contexts. Avoid in standard UI — it reads as outside the palette and breaks tonal cohesion.

black.$description: Pure black. Used as the background.default override for the .art site (photo-optimized, no green undertone needed). Not the default canvas for other properties — that is green.950.
